prompt:
Repo convention says Format-Table is banned anywhere near a pinned output (its rendering shifts with terminal width and pwsh version), but the capacity review doc still needs fixed-width text tables. So: hand-rolled renderer, `textgrid.ps1` in the repo root, dot-sourceable, one function. And yes, the test file scans your source — Format-Table/Format-List/Format-Wide (and the `ft` alias) and Out-String are all rejected.

`Format-TextGrid -Rows <object[]> [-MaxWidth <int>]` — Rows is an array of objects with typed properties (the callers build [pscustomobject]s), returns the whole table as ONE [string].

- Columns come from the FIRST row's properties, in property order. Later rows are read by those names.
- A column is right-aligned when the first row's value for it is a number (any of the integer types, single, double, decimal); everything else is left-aligned. A STRING that happens to look numeric ('1042') is left-aligned — type decides, not shape. Headers align the same way as their column's data.
- Cell text: $null renders as empty; numbers render invariant-culture ToString (a [double] 12.0 renders '12', 3.5 renders '3.5'); strings render as-is.
- -MaxWidth, when given, must be at least 4 or throw 'textgrid: MaxWidth must be at least 4'. Any rendered text longer than MaxWidth — headers included — becomes its first (MaxWidth - 3) characters plus '...'.
- Column width = the widest rendered text in the column (header included) after truncation. Two spaces between columns. After the header row comes a rule row: '-' repeated to each column's width, same separator.
- No line may carry trailing whitespace — pad, then trim the right edge of every line. Lines join with LF and there is no trailing newline after the last line.
- Empty Rows: throw 'textgrid: no rows' — OUR message, not a parameter-binding error; think about what Mandatory does to an empty array before it ever reaches your code.

Acceptance: `pwsh -NoProfile -File test_textgrid.ps1` from the workspace root must pass unmodified — don't touch the test file.
notes: 6P2 item 5: object array -> fixed-width text table, column autosize with MaxWidth truncation '...', numeric right-align, hand-rendered with Format-* banned by source scan (incl. word-boundary ft-alias check). PowerShell-native pins: input is OBJECTS with typed properties — alignment decided by the first row's .NET value TYPE ('1042' string stays left, [long] right), [double] invariant rendering probed (12.0 -> '12', 0.25 -> '0.25'), first-row PSObject.Properties order as column order, [AllowEmptyCollection] needed on -Rows so the pinned 'textgrid: no rows' message can fire instead of a binding error. Differentiation vs bash-fixedw (named sibling): that one formats CSV TEXT with awk-style width math; here the contract is object introspection + type-driven alignment, which has no text-pipeline analog. Expected grids in the harness are assembled from the same width/alignment rules via -f, with TrimEnd pinning the no-trailing-whitespace law. Domain (capacity-review host/build tables) collides with nothing existing.
